pages.text: Fire curing exposes tobacco to smoke and heat from smoldering fires over an extended period. This creates a darker, heavier, and more assertive character than other curing methods.

If implemented, fire curing should feel like the boldest and most aggressive curing style. The reward is rich, smoky tobacco with a strong presence. The risk is overexposure, harshness, and quality loss if the leaf is mishandled.
Fire-cured leaf works well in strong pipe tobaccos, darker blends, and specialty products where body and aroma matter more than sweetness. It should not behave like a universal best option.
A good fire cure should demand fuel, time, and control. It should produce distinct results that justify the extra effort.
